growth_records.strain: not specified (with lytic phage 2972; CRISPR model system)

up.units: ug consumed per cell (e, resource consumption rate of growing bacteria)

growth_records.cite: Weissman et al. Immune loss as a driver of coexistence during host-phage coevolution. ISME J (2018). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.194. [PMC5776473]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum bacterial growth rate | v | 1.4 divisions/hr ... Resource pool concentration | A | 350 microg/ml ... Resource consumption rate of growing bacteria | e | 5x10^7 microg/cell
growth_records.notes: Table 1 model parameters for S. thermophilus. Reported max growth rate v=1.4 divisions/hr (=doublings per hour), so doubling_time=1/1.4=0.714 h; mu=ln2*1.4=0.693*1.4=0.970 /h. These are literature-sourced model parameters (rationale in Supplementary Text S2), not fresh measurements in this paper; flagged low confidence. Chemostat flow rate w=0.3 ml/hr given but culture volume not stated, so dilution rate per h not derivable. Half-saturation z=1 microg/ml; resource pool A=350 microg/ml. Uptake e=5e7 microg per new cell is a stoichiometric consumption constant, not a specific rate in mmol/gDW/h.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with lactose as carbon source; preculture in M17 (lactose)
growth_records.cite: Pismennoi et al. Development and Optimisation of HILIC-LC-MS Method for Determination of Carbohydrates in Fermentation Samples. Molecules (2021). DOI:10.3390/molecules26123669. [PMC8235229]
growth_records.snippet: Streptococcus thermophilus was inoculated into fresh M17 medium (lactose as carbon source) ... inoculated into a bioreactor containing 300 mL of CDM ... CDM contained either sucrose or lactose as a carbon source. ... After 7 h of batch grow
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, lactose-CDM condition. mu = D = 0.25 h-1 at chemostat steady state (steady-state assumption); doubling_time = ln2/0.25 = 0.693/0.25 = 2.77 h (calculated). This is an analytical-method (HILIC-LC-MS) paper: it defines the S. thermophilus chemostat conditions and quantifies fructose/glucose/galactose/sucrose/lactose in the harvested samples, but no per-sample saccharide concentrations, uptake or secretion RATES are tabulated in the main text (Table 2 lists only sample names/time points/carbon source), so no uptake/secretion values are extractable. Temperature and pH not stated in the bioreactor description. CDM full composition not given. Growth anaerobic under N2 / N2:CO2 (80:20).

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with sucrose as carbon source; preculture in M17 (lactose)
growth_records.cite: Pismennoi et al. Development and Optimisation of HILIC-LC-MS Method for Determination of Carbohydrates in Fermentation Samples. Molecules (2021). DOI:10.3390/molecules26123669. [PMC8235229]
growth_records.snippet: Streptococcus thermophilus was inoculated into fresh M17 medium (lactose as carbon source) ... inoculated into a bioreactor containing 300 mL of CDM ... CDM contained either sucrose or lactose as a carbon source. ... After 7 h of batch grow
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, sucrose-CDM condition. mu = D = 0.25 h-1 at chemostat steady state; doubling_time = ln2/0.25 = 2.77 h (calculated). Same run design as the lactose condition (both carbon sources tested). Analytical-method paper: no per-sample saccharide concentrations or uptake/secretion rates tabulated in the main text (Table 2 lists sample names/time points/carbon source only), so no rate values extractable. Temperature and pH not stated; CDM full composition not given; anaerobic under N2 / N2:CO2 (80:20).

growth_records.cite: Stulova et al. Microcalorimetric study of the growth of Streptococcus thermophilus in renneted milk. Frontiers in Microbiology (2015). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00079. [PMC4322847]
growth_records.snippet: Growth characteristics of Streptococcus thermophilus ST12 in RSM and r-RSM at 40 C (Table 1): mu_max1 and mu_max2 per inoculation rate; initial pH = 6.47.
growth_records.notes: Table 1, RSM (liquid reconstituted skim milk), inoculation 1e6 CFU/mL, 40C, batch microcalorimetry. mu_max1 (first exponential phase)=1.88 h-1 reported as primary growth_rate; doubling=ln2/1.88=0.369 h. mu_max2 (second exponential phase)=1.05 h-1. Growth rate derived calorimetrically as slope of ln(dQ/dt) vs t (heat-flow proportional to biomass), heat yield Yq=4.45e-9 J/CFU, cell mass 0.2e-12 g. Values labeled Wh-1 in table are h-1. Carbohydrate/lactate/amino-acid consumption measured only at 1e5 CFU/mL, not at this inoculation rate.

up.units: mmol/L net consumed by end of 2nd exponential phase (NOT per-time)

sec.units: mmol/L produced by end of 2nd exponential phase (NOT per-time)

growth_records.cite: Stulova et al. Microcalorimetric study of the growth of Streptococcus thermophilus in renneted milk. Frontiers in Microbiology (2015). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00079. [PMC4322847]
growth_records.snippet: Growth characteristics of Streptococcus thermophilus ST12 in RSM and r-RSM at 40 C (Table 1): mu_max1 and mu_max2 per inoculation rate; initial pH = 6.47.
growth_records.notes: Table 1, RSM (liquid reconstituted skim milk), inoculation 1e5 CFU/mL, 40C, batch microcalorimetry. mu_max1 (first exponential phase)=1.85 h-1 reported as primary growth_rate; doubling=ln2/1.85=0.375 h. mu_max2 (second exponential phase)=1.18 h-1. Growth rate derived calorimetrically as slope of ln(dQ/dt) vs t (heat-flow proportional to biomass), heat yield Yq=4.45e-9 J/CFU, cell mass 0.2e-12 g. Values labeled Wh-1 in table are h-1. Carbohydrate/lactate and amino-acid data measured only at 1e5 CFU/mL: ~130 mmol/L lactose hydrolysed, 127 mmol/L glucose and 58 mmol/L galactose consumed, ~20 mmol/L lactate produced by end of 2nd exponential phase; lactose consumption per biomass 2200 mmol/gdw; lactate yield per lactose <0.2 mol/mol (theoretical max 4); 4.6 mmol/g amino acids into biomass (30% from free AA, ~25% from Glu transamination, ~45% from peptides). These are concentration changes / per-biomass values, NOT per-time rates.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14786 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14786
